title: [FIRRTL] ModulePrefixAnnotation not working properly on Vec size 1 typed smems
body:
Similar issue to https://github.com/llvm/circt/issues/7788.

Consider the following FIRRTL:
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Top : %[[
  {
    "class":"chisel3.ModulePrefixAnnotation",
    "target": "~Top|Top>ram",
    "prefix":"Foo_"
  }
]]
  public module Top :
    input clock : Clock
    input raddr : UInt<6>
    input waddr : UInt<6>
    input wdata : UInt<8>[1]
    output rdata : UInt<8>[1]

    smem ram : UInt<8>[1][64]

    write mport w = ram[waddr], clock
    connect w, wdata

    node readAddr = raddr ; Avoid https://github.com/llvm/circt/issues/7834
    read mport r = ram[readAddr], clock
    connect rdata, r
```
If you run this with Firtool 1.93.1, you will get:
```verilog
module ram_0_64x8(
...
endmodule
module Top(
...
  ram_0_64x8 ram_0_ext (
  ...
endmodule
```

If you remove all of the `[1]` (to make the data type of the smem no longer a Vec of size 1), the resulting memory will be prefixed as expected:
```verilog
module Foo_ram_64x8(
...
endmodule
module Top(
...
  Foo_ram_64x8 ram_ext (
  ...
endmodule
```

**Note** this only applies to Vecs of size 1, things work properly if the Vec is size 2 or larger.
